fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong analyst backing and impressive recent returns, but faces valuation concerns and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 4.6% (EPS 2.37 vs 2.27); next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY and analyst estimates of 236.6% for the current quarter and 300.7% for the fiscal year indicate substantial expansion.
Operating margin of 21.8% and profit margin of 17.7% demonstrate strong profitability.
Return on equity at 22.8% suggests efficient use of shareholder capital.
Mean analyst target of $1104.68 implies significant upside potential from the current price.
Consensus 'buy' rating from 24 analysts supports a positive outlook.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 146.41 and Forward P/E of 45.94 are very high, indicating a premium valuation.
Price/Book of 70.14 and Price/Sales (TTM) of 26.00 also suggest the stock is richly valued.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44 indicates a significant reliance on debt financing.
Free cash flow of $93.35M (TTM) is modest relative to the company's market capitalization.
A recent report from GuruFocus suggests the stock is still overvalued despite a recent price drop.

news.paragraph: Lumentum Holdings (LITE) saw its shares drop 4.6% following a GuruFocus report suggesting it remains overvalued, despite a recent Seeking Alpha article highlighting its impressive 135% year-to-date returns, significantly outpacing Nvidia. This comes as an analyst upgrade was noted, with purchase obligations exploding, indicating strong demand in the AI networking space. However, the company's valuation metrics and a recent downgrade from a Seeking Alpha analyst suggest a mixed outlook.

headlines.title: Lumentum Holdings Inc (LITE) Shares Fall 4.6% -- GF Value Says Still Overvalued
Lumentum: An AI Networking Stock That Beat Nvidia By 7X YTD For Returns Of 135% YTD (LITE)
Stocks In AI Bottleneck Soar 1,000%. Now Nvidia Is Demanding 20X More Supply
SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: NVDA, TXN, LITE, ZS
Lumentum: Purchase Obligations Are Exploding And Here's Why That Matters (Rating Upgrade)

examples.body: $LITE I can feel a rebound coming. This will take off again as soon as this stock splits and new investors jump in. You know it's coming, don't be late.
